**History of Present Illness**
Robert is experiencing increased fatigue, nocturia (two to three times a night), and polydipsia (more thirst than usual). These symptoms are consistent with poor glycemic control as indicated by his lab results. His Hemoglobin A1c (A1c) is 9.2%, an increase from 7.4% six months ago. At home, he has had blood sugars in the 200s, sometimes higher, but he ran out of test strips and hasn't been testing as often.

Robert is on metformin 1,000 milligrams twice daily but has missed doses while traveling last month. His diet and exercise have declined due to caregiving responsibilities, resulting in a weight gain of 15 pounds to a current weight of 240 pounds (height 5'9", BMI 35.4).
